what_it_does: Defines window.MS_KINDS, a central registry of declarative producible design parts called kinds. Each kind belongs to a category, carries metadata, and exposes a typed variable schema. The file provides accessor functions to list, retrieve, group, and present kinds as selection options.
observations: Kinds are grouped into eight categories: surface, component, style, motion, guidance, input, media, logic.
Each kind declares variables with key, label, type, source, def, and optional extra fields like options or of.
Variable types are token, color, enum, number, text, ref, media, bool.
Variable sources are default, preference, memory, ai, explicit, implying a resolution priority.
A reusable v() helper builds variable-slot objects to keep declarations terse.
An ORDER array fixes stable display order and attaches id onto each kind object.
asOptions() turns kinds into {id,label,desc,icon} shapes for guided interview cards.
The file explicitly avoids hardcoding token or status values; token variables reference token registries by family name.
Unknown kind ids trigger console.error and return null; unknown category ids log and fall back to the raw id.
Recent additions include deck, look, notification, data_source, and flow kinds.
standouts: The 'universal systems with variables; instance applications from declarative types' design principle.
asOptions() makes adding a kind automatically surface it in the guided interview without per-type UI.
Variable source labels encode where a value should come from: default, preference, memory, ai, or explicit.
The catalog is intended to mirror the kind table in db/schema.sql.
conventions: Uses an IIFE to namespace the registry on window.
Separates static data (CATEGORIES, KINDS, ORDER) from accessor functions.
Uses Object.assign in v() to merge optional extra properties.
Attaches ids programmatically from ORDER rather than repeating them in each kind definition.
Loud failure via console.error for unknown kind/category lookups.
concerns: The v() builder does not expose a first-class way to set required, options, or of; these must be passed in extra.
There is no runtime validation that every kind's category exists in CATEGORIES.
asOptions() silently returns an empty array for an unknown category instead of logging an error.
The documented required? flag is not represented in the variable schema builder or kind declarations.
Ref/of constraints are declared but not enforced within this file.
KINDS and ORDER could drift if a kind is added to one but not the other.
notes: This is the studio's declarative parts catalog: it enumerates every producible kind, its category, its variables, and helper accessors that feed the generative UI pipeline.
questions: How are token families registered and resolved for variables of type token?
What component consumes asOptions() and how does it map icon string values to actual icon assets?
Is there a separate validator that checks variable-slot shape and required/options/of consistency?
How does the AI source resolution actually produce values?
Where are user preferences stored and how do preference-sourced variables bind to that store?
Are required? and options? consumed downstream, or are they only documentation?
How is this file kept in sync with the kind table in db/schema.sql?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it catalogs kinds with variable schemas and provides accessors for listing, retrieving, and presenting them.
apparent_themes: Universal type system for UI artifacts
Self-describing catalog to avoid per-type UI code
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish a single, exhaustive taxonomy of producible design parts so the studio can generate any artifact by resolving its declared variables rather than hand-coding each type.
novelty: It treats every UI artifact as an instance of a declarative kind with typed, sourced variable slots, and auto-generates interview options from the same catalog so new kinds appear in the UI automatically.
contribution: It contributes the kind taxonomy, variable vocabulary, category ordering, and registry accessors that a fused generative studio would keep to enumerate and instantiate design parts generically.
summary_for_embedding: A JavaScript registry (window.MS_KINDS) that defines a categorized catalog of declarative producible design kinds, their typed variable slots with source/default metadata, stable ordering, and accessor functions for listing, retrieving, and presenting kinds as selection options.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the studio's universal spine for generative UI by describing every producible part and its configurable variables so instances can be resolved and composed automatically.

symbols.does: Builds a variable-slot object from key, label, type, source, def, and optional extra properties.
symbols.description: A terse factory for kind variable declarations. It returns an object with the core fields and merges any additional metadata such as options or of via Object.assign.

symbols.does: Returns all kinds in stable display order.
symbols.description: Maps the ORDER array over the KINDS object to produce a sequentially ordered list of kind definitions, preserving the intended catalog presentation.

symbols.does: Retrieves a kind by id, logging an error and returning null if unknown.
symbols.description: Looks up a kind in KINDS by its string id. If the id is not present, it emits a console.error listing known ids and returns null, implementing the loud-failure convention.

symbols.does: Returns a shallow copy of the category list.
symbols.description: Exposes the CATEGORIES array via slice() so callers can enumerate the display categories without mutating the internal data.

symbols.does: Returns the human-readable label for a category id.
symbols.description: Finds the matching category in CATEGORIES and returns its label. If not found, it logs an error and returns the raw id as a fallback.

symbols.does: Groups all kinds by their category id.
symbols.description: Initializes a bucket for each category, then distributes every kind into the bucket matching its category property, returning a map from category id to kind array.

symbols.does: Returns the variable schema for a given kind id.
symbols.description: Uses get() to fetch a kind and returns its variables array, or an empty array if the kind is unknown. This isolates variable access behind the registry API.

symbols.does: Transforms kinds into selection-option shapes for UI cards.
symbols.description: Accepts an optional category filter, maps the relevant kinds to {id, label, desc, icon} objects, and supplies defaults for summary and icon so the guided interview can render them without per-kind UI code.
